title: begonia elisabethae
intro: Begonia elisabethae, also known as Begonia, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to the tropical rainforests of Southeast Asia. The plant's rhizomes and leaves are traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia, for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Begonia elisabethae include reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various ailments. In herbal preparations, Begonia elisabethae is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its availability and usage are strictly limited due to its endangered status.
